items.title: Video shows law enforcement running toward White House Correspondents dinner suspect
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A man with guns and knives tried to rush past the security perimeter inside the Washington hotel where President Donald Trump was about to address the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

items.title: Law enforcement respond after shooting incident at White House Correspondents' dinner
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner attended by President Donald Trump on Saturday night, charging toward the ballroom in a chaotic encounter with Secret Service agents as guests dived under tables at the sound of shots being fired.</article>

items.content:
<article>Russian State Duma Speaker Vyacheslav Volodin said on Sunday that it was a “great honor” to take part in an inauguration ceremony of a military memorial in Pyongyang.

items.title: Sultan of Oman meets with Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met on Sunday with the Sultan of Oman, Haitham bin Tariq Al Said, in Muscat. Oman's foreign minister, Badr al Busaidi, also attended the meeting.

#iran #oman #news

items.title: DC mayor says 'no reason to believe' anyone else involved in dinner shooting incident
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The mayor of Washington D.C. said that authorities had "no reason to believe" anyone else was "involved" after a man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside a high-profile journalists' dinner attended by President Donald Trump.

In a news conference on Saturday, Muriel Bowser thanked law enforcement "who did their jobs tonight and made sure that all guests were safe."

#shorts #murielbowser #dc #trump #shooting</article>

items.title: Trump says being president a 'dangerous profession'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump struck a somber tone as he addressed reporters at the White House, saying being president is “a dangerous profession” and that attempted violence against him is “part of the job.” This came after a man with guns and knives tried to rush past the security perimeter inside the Washington hotel where Trump was about to address the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

#shorts #trump #whitehouse #briefing #shooting</article>

items.title: AP bureau chief describes what happened at the White House Correspondents' dinner
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner Saturday night unfolded just outside the ballroom holding President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance, cabinet members and thousands of dinner guests, disrupting minutes after it began an annual event.

AP Washington bureau chief Anna Johnson shared what she saw.

#shorts #whitehouse #trump #vance #shooting</article>

items.title: AP sees inside Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant 40 years after disaster
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The two explosions at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant came decades apart. The first, on April 26, 1986, spread a cloud of deadly radiation that raised fears across Europe. Ukrainian officials say the second, on Feb. 14, 2025, was caused by a Russian drone.

#shorts #chernobyl #nuclear #powerplant #anniversary #ukraine</article>

items.title: DC mayor says 'no reason to believe' anyone else involved in White House dinner shooting
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In a news conference on Saturday, Muriel Bowser thanked law enforcement "who did their jobs tonight and made sure that all guests were safe." Earlier, Trump was uninjured and hustled away after the suspect rushed toward the annual dinner of the White House correspondents' association. Authorities said the suspect was swarmed by Secret Service agents and taken into custody outside the ballroom where the event was being held.

#trump #whitehouse #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Watch the moment Trump gets rushed off stage at the White House correspondents' dinner
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner attended by President Donald Trump on Saturday night, charging toward the ballroom in a chaotic encounter with Secret Service agents as guests dived under tables at the sound of shots being fired.

The president was uninjured and was rushed off the stage.

#whitehouse #trump #dinner #dc</article>

items.title: How the shooting incident at White House correspondents' dinner unfolded
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump was unharmed and other top White House officials were evacuated from an annual dinner of the White House Correspondents' Association after a man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby and opened fire. The shooting suspect was taken into custody and identified as Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of Torrance, California. A motive was not immediately known. (Produced by Luke Garratt)

#trump #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Explosive device kills 13 and injures 38 on a bus in Colombia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least 14 people were killed when an explosive device detonated as a bus passed by on a highway in southwestern Colombia on Saturday. Almost 40 other people were injured in the explosion on the Pan-American Highway in the El Túnel sector of Cajibío, including five minors. (AP Video: Juan Diaz)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: LIVE: Outside suspect’s home after White House correspondents’ dinner shooting incident
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Live view from outside the home of Cole Tomas Allen in Torrance, California, after the 31-year-old armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside a high-profile journalists’ dinner attended by President Donald Trump and multiple senior U.S. leaders. Secret Service agents swarmed the suspect and took him into custody while the president was uninjured. Read more: https://bit.ly/4t0BtLI

#live</article>
